name: Los Melonares Climb Loop
name_original: Desde Villanueva del Río y Minas a Castilblanco de los Arroyos – circular por Cantillana, Villaverde del Río y Burguillos
description: This moderate 54.1-mile road cycling route explores the Sierra Norte de Sevilla, featuring towns and natural landscapes.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Villanueva Del Río y Minas traverse a diverse landscape at the foothills of the Sierra Morena and bordering the Vega del Guadalquivir. This region offers varied terrain, from challenging climbs in the Sierra Morena foothills to flatter sections along the Vega del Guadalquivir. Cyclists encounter olive groves, dehesas, and holm oaks, with the Rivera del Huéznar also providing scenic routes. The area's topography supports a range of road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Villanueva Del Río y Minas The most popular road cycling route is Los Melonares Climb Loop, a 54.1 miles (87.1 km) trail that takes 4 hours 3 minutes to complete. This route offers significant elevation gain through the Sierra Morena foothills. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is From Villanueva del Río y Minas to Lora del Río – Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park – circular, a moderate 39.7 miles (63.9 km) path. This route explores the natural park's varied landscapes and river valleys. Local road cyclists also love the Venta Hermanos Moyano – Piedra del Moro loop from Tocina, a 35.0 miles (56.4 km) trail leading through rolling terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 16 minutes. Road cycling around Villanueva Del Río y Minas is defined by Sierra Morena foothills, Vega del Guadalquivir plains, and the Rivera del Huéznar valley. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Villanueva Del Río y Minas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 1200 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villanueva Del Río y Minas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Villanueva Del Río y Minas?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes available around Villanueva Del Río y Minas, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in this region?
A: The region offers varied terrain, from challenging climbs and panoramic views in the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra Morena foothills&lt;/strong&gt; to flatter, more relaxed sections along the &lt;strong&gt;Vega del Guadalquivir&lt;/strong&gt;. You'll also find scenic routes traversing the &lt;strong&gt;Rivera del Huéznar&lt;/strong&gt; valley, passing through olive groves, dehesas, and holm oaks.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those seeking an easy ride?
A: Yes, there are several easy road cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31846269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcolea del Río – Lora del Río loop from Alcolea del Río&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 47.4 km route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20123288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcolea del Río – Old locomotive loop from Villanueva del Río y Minas&lt;/a&gt; offers another accessible option.

Q: Where can I find a challenging road cycling route with significant climbs?
A: For a more challenging experience with notable elevation gain, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994035628/los-melonares-climb-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Melonares Climb Loop&lt;/a&gt;. This 87.1 km route takes you through the Sierra Morena foothills and features nearly 900 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Many of the routes in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995884183/from-villanueva-del-rio-y-minas-to-lora-del-rio-sierra-norte-de-sevilla-natural-park-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Villanueva del Río y Minas to Lora del Río – Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park – circular&lt;/a&gt;, which explores varied landscapes over 63.9 km.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along road cycling routes?
A: You can integrate visits to historical sites into your rides. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4782188" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Archaeological Site of Mulva-Munigua&lt;/a&gt;, a significant Roman city, is nearby. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5316001" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva del Río y Minas Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, offering a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: Are there any scenic natural features to discover while cycling?
A: Absolutely. The area is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle near the &lt;strong&gt;Rivera del Huéznar&lt;/strong&gt;, explore routes that pass by the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2676625" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lago Azul&lt;/a&gt; (Blue Lake), or enjoy views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2676623" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéznar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. The Sierra Morena foothills themselves provide stunning natural backdrops.

Q: When is the best time of year for road cycling in Villanueva Del Río y Minas?
A: The best time for road cycling in Villanueva Del Río y Minas is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder and more comfortable for longer rides, avoiding the intense heat of summer.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Villanueva Del Río y Minas?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from challenging climbs to scenic river valleys, and the opportunity to explore both natural landscapes and historical sites.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park?
A: Yes, a notable route that takes you through this beautiful protected area is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995884183/from-villanueva-del-rio-y-minas-to-lora-del-rio-sierra-norte-de-sevilla-natural-park-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Villanueva del Río y Minas to Lora del Río – Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park – circular&lt;/a&gt;. This route allows you to experience the varied landscapes and river valleys within the park.

Q: What is a good moderate-difficulty road cycling route for exploring rolling terrain?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Venta Hermanos Moyano – Piedra del Moro loop from Tocina&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 56.4 km route that leads through rolling terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 16 minutes, offering a balanced ride.
tips: The lake is located near the town center, a few meters from the Iron Bridge over the Rivera del Huéznar.

The Los Melonares slope, located in the Natural Park of the Sierra Norte de Sevilla, is one of the most classic Seville ascents. A constant climb of almost 5 km and an average slope of 5.5%, reaching up to 10%. Do not forget to recharge the drums before starting to climb.

This historic complex is one of the 'witnesses' to the mining activity of coal exploitation in the area, which until 1972 was the essence of the municipality. In 2018, the Industrial Heritage Laboratory VRM.LAB_Pozo 5 was inaugurated in the facilities of the old well. It is a space created for research, teaching and dissemination to contribute to the promotion of Industrial Heritage.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty and terrain like for this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as a 'Moderate road ride' and requires good fitness. While primarily a road cycling route, it's important to be aware that some segments may be unpaved and more challenging to ride, requiring adaptable cycling skills and equipment. You'll experience varied terrain with an elevation gain and descent of approximately 900 meters.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route covers a distance of 87.1 kilometers and has an estimated cycling time of 4 hours and 4 minutes. This duration is for cycling only and does not include stops for rest, food, or sightseeing.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to cycle this route in the Seville province?
faqs.answer: The best times to cycle in the Seville province, including this route, are generally spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder and more comfortable for cycling. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler and wetter.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or natural features along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers a blend of natural beauty and cultural interest. You'll pass through natural landscapes like the Arenales del Parroso Nature Reserve and the impressive scenery of the Sierra Morena foothills. Highlights along the way include Lago Azul, the challenging Cuesta de Los Melonares, and the Historic Mining Complex "Pozo No. 5".

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with sections of other official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Mtb Vía de la Plata and the Vía de la Plata hiking - 01 Sevilla to Merida for short segments, as well as the [CIMA SV02] Los Melonares.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access public transport to start this route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Villanueva del Río y Minas. You can find public parking within the town. For public transport, Villanueva del Río y Minas has a train station on the C3 Cercanías line from Seville, which typically allows bikes, though it's always best to check current regulations with Renfe.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route, especially within the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle this road route. While parts of the area are within the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park, general road cycling on public roads does not typically require special permits. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected natural areas.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for cycling with dogs?
faqs.answer: Road cycling routes, especially those with potentially unpaved sections and traffic, are generally not recommended for cycling with dogs. It's safer for your dog and other road users to choose dedicated dog-friendly trails or areas where they can run freely and safely.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food, drinks, or accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through several towns including Villanueva del Río y Minas, Cantillana, Villaverde del Río, Burguillos, and Castilblanco de los Arroyos, you will find opportunities for cafes, pubs, and accommodation. These towns offer convenient stops to refuel and rest.

faqs.question: What should I be aware of regarding the potentially unpaved sections mentioned for this route?
faqs.answer: While primarily a road cycling route, the mention of 'some segments of this route may be unpaved and difficult to ride' suggests you should be prepared for varied surfaces. This could mean gravel, dirt, or rougher patches. Consider using tires that can handle mixed terrain, and be ready to adjust your speed and technique accordingly. It adds an element of adventure, but also requires vigilance.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect to see while cycling this route?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse and impressive scenery. The route takes you through the natural beauty of the Sierra Morena foothills, part of the Vega del Guadalquivir and Guadalquivir-Doñana tourist region. You'll see natural landscapes, pass through charming Spanish countryside towns, and experience the varied terrain of the Seville province.
